elements.title: Mortimer War Memorial
elements.contents: ... that the Mortimer War Memorial (pictured) was erected three years after the end of the First World War, but a plaque honouring those who died in the Second World War was not added until 1999?

elements.title: Emily Manning
elements.contents: ... that Emily Manning used a poetic dialogue between a husband and his clairvoyant wife to examine Victorian gender roles?

elements.title: police officials in ancient Egypt
elements.contents: ... that most high-ranking police officials in ancient Egypt during the Ptolemaic period were Greek?

elements.title: Paul Gorton
elements.contents: ... that Paul Gorton was booked as a guest on Do You Know Your Place?, but ended up co-hosting the show instead?

elements.title: an Indonesian museum
elements.contents: ... that an Indonesian museum that receives c. 100,000 visitors a year was built in a graveyard parking lot?

elements.title: Thomas Henry Tracy
elements.contents: ... that Thomas Henry Tracy, the city engineer of London and Vancouver, was also a lieutenant colonel who fought in the North-West Rebellion?

elements.title: an orgy on a Kyiv hill
elements.contents: ... that 15,000 Ukrainians joined a Telegram channel for an orgy on a Kyiv hill in the event of a Russian nuclear strike?

elements.title: Kurtöp language
elements.contents: ... that the Kurtöp language has a word for an assortment of sounds like "hard, non-spherical object[s] rolling down a paved road"?

elements.title: fought with clowns and lit their circus on fire
elements.contents: ... that Toronto firefighters once fought with clowns and lit their circus on fire?
